I initiated two brand new positions in the growth portfolio a couple weeks ago to expand my AI hardware bucket: 

• $AAOI ($79.43) +85%
• $CRDO ($94.97) +70%

Applied Optoelectronics builds lasers and optical transceivers that move data faster across AI clusters.

Credo built its business on the copper interconnect layer inside AI racks but today’s $750M DustPhotonics acquisition gives it a credible path into optical as AI networks scale to faster and more demanding architectures.

Together, both are bets on the data movement layer of AI where connectivity matters just as much as compute as clusters scale.

$GOOGL just rolled out Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 which is designed to help robots better reason about the physical world.

The upgrade improves visual understanding, spatial reasoning and a robot’s ability to tell when a job is done.
$IONQ is up more than 20% after winning a DARPA contract tied to next-gen quantum networking
BITCOIN JUST CROSSED $75,000

$META CEO Mark Zuckerberg has reportedly moved his desk into the AI lab to work directly with Alexandr Wang and Nat Friedman.

He is also said to be coding throughout the day as Meta pushes harder on AI.

$IONQ won a DARPA contract to help build quantum networking tech using synthetic diamond materials for quantum memory.

The goal is to help different types of quantum computers connect and work together which could matter if quantum needs to scale through networks.

$GOOGL is reportedly in talks with $MRVL on TPU development with Marvell potentially taking on a design-services role similar to MediaTek.

It is also said to be involved in early discussions around a dedicated Google LLM inference chip.

$AMZN ~$12B deal for $GSAT would take Project Kuiper beyond broadband giving Amazon licensed spectrum & a D2D path without relying on carriers.

If AI is going to live in robots, devices & physical infrastructure then connectivity becomes part of the stack too and Amazon may be moving early to own it.

• PPI 0.5% MoM vs. Est. 1.1%

• PPI Core 3.8% YoY vs. Est. 4.1%
• PPI Core 0.1% MoM vs. Est. 0.4%

$AMZN and $AAPL have signed an agreement to use Amazon’s LEO network for satellite features across iPhone and Apple Watch.

That support is expected to include services like Emergency SOS.
$AMZN TO ACQUIRE $GSAT TO EXPAND ITS LEO SATELLITE NETWORK

$CRDO is up over 20% after acquiring DustPhotonics for $750M which is a deal that significantly expands its position in optical connectivity.

CEO William Brennan said the deal helps build a vertically integrated platform spanning copper to optical and chip to cluster.

Initiated Credo in the growth portfolio a couple weeks ago when the market was still handing it out in the $80s.

$UBER is expanding its $LCID robotaxi deal to at least 35,000 vehicles for its future global autonomous fleet.

Uber is also investing another $200M in Lucid bringing its total investment to $500M as Lucid secures $1.05B in fresh funding.

$AMZN is said to be nearing a deal for $GSAT as it pushes deeper into satellite connectivity to rival Starlink.

Bloomberg says the deal could be announced as soon as tomorrow.

The MOVE Index is "the bond market VIX." It measures the implied volatility of 30-day options from the 2-year note to the 30-year bond.

Bond volatility has been crashing over the last 2 weeks. This is consistent with the collapse in TLT volume shown above.

TLT is a good proxy for bond market interest. And interest (volume) is now at a 3+ year low.

* $TLT's volume today was 12.720M shares, the lowest daily volume since January 2023 (and this INCLUDES holidays)

* The 5-day average volume is 17.974M, the lowest since April 2023.
*S&P 500 RISES 1%, SET TO ERASE LOSSES SINCE IRAN WAR BEGAN

Private credit was always on a search for companies that had a big moat (meaning competitors could not easily disrupt them) and stable cash flows.

For years companies that fit this criteria were Software/SaaS companies. There are tens of thousands of them that makes specialized software for every industry and a multitude of applications. So private credit funds have a big weighting in Software/SaaS companies.

Then AI came and disrupted the Software/SaaS industry. Now the cost of making software is collapsing towards zero and the fear is these companies are going to get disrupted by low cost entrants (smaller/no moat) offering the same Software/SaaS products at a fraction of the price (uncertain cash flows).

That said, Software/SaaS companies haven’t been disrupted yet. So when the public companies release  their Q1 earnings reports, they will show their moats and cash flows are still very much intact.

But this is an issue of where the Software/SaaS companies are going next, not where they are now.

Publicly traded Software/SaaS stocks have been getting crushed in the last few months on fears that these companies are about to experience huge disruption thanks to AI.

The private credit funds are in a difficult spot. They value these companies based on where they are now, not necessarily an expectation of where they’re going next. 

Investors in private credit funds think these companies are in trouble, so if private credit funds CORRECTLY say that their valuation RIGHT NOW is still top dollar (because it is), then private credit investors will take that valuation and run for the hills. This is why these funds are seeing huge requests for redemption.

It’s a difficult thing to do, which is why they have a problem in simultaneously telling everyone that the cash flows of these companies are great NOW, and also convinced them that they will be great going FORWARD in a world of abundant AI.

Before the war, the world was producing about 100M barrels of oil a day, and consuming about 100M barrels of oil a day.

Now the world is producing somewhere between 85M and 90M barrels of oil a day. That means somebody has to stop consuming 10M to 15M barrels of oil a day. Economists call this demand destruction.

The market has a very efficient way to get to this reduced level of demand, it raises the price forcing marginal buyers to stop consuming oil.

But if government subsidize the current high price of oil, the demand does not change and the market price has to go higher in order to get that demand destruction.

I’m all in favor of lower oil prices like everybody else, but we’ve got a supply demand imbalance that needs to be brought into equilibrium. 

So either open the Strait so we can get back to 100M of oil a day, or figure out another way to tell the equivalent of 10m to 15M barrels of oil a day that they cannot go to the gas station to fill up their car.

A legendary investor, Murray was also a good man. Our heartfelt condolences go out to his wife, their children, and everyone at @HorizonKinetics. They are good people and this is a profound loss.

We will always appreciate the opportunities @KeithMcCullough had to sit down with Murray at both Hedgeye Investing Summit and Hedgeye Live. It was an honor to hear directly from one of the great long-term investors of his generation.

Rest in peace, Murray.

📺 $SNDK VS. $MU & $TSLA BOUNCE TRADE

$SNDK is now extended, and the proper entries were earlier near support when risk was defined. Chasing here offers poor reward. 

$MU presents a cleaner setup with continued upside, which is why capital is rotating there, and I am trading it.

The key focus now is execution: trimming into strength, locking gains, and avoiding the trap of giving profits back. 

Markets have pushed higher for several days, increasing the likelihood of a short-term pullback. 

The $TSLA trade highlights this approach—entering when fear was elevated, then scaling out into strength, and now trimming.

Remember, $TSLA is still a broken name, so it is just a tradable rally.

$SPY $QQQ $IWM $GLD $IBIT

📺 THE EASY MONEY HAS BEEN MADE — TIME TO REDUCE EXPOSURE

We just went through a full market cycle shift:
– Market dropped ~9.8% from highs (a meaningful pullback)
– Oscillator hit around -60 (deeply oversold conditions)
– That created a high-probability turning point

The key moment was when a gap-down driven by an oil spike (a fear event) was absorbed by the market rather than collapsing.

That’s when I shifted from defensive to offensive: moved from day trading/tactical to active swing longs and started building positions with conviction.

*

Now the situation has flipped. Oscillator is +60 to +65 (overbought), and the market is approaching resistance.

So, the easy money is already made, and risk/reward is now worse.

*

My Plan

I am trimming positions, locking in gains, and not initiating aggressive new longs.

Today is not the day to jump in. Instead, trail winners, reduce exposure, and let weaker hands get shaken out.

I am even considering shorting $SPY slightly as a hedge. Not bearish — just protective positioning.

*

Oil still matters, but recently less tightly correlated with equities.

Still reacting to geopolitical headlines and ceasefire uncertainty.

Key level: $USO around $123 (still elevated).

Oil is no longer a perfect market driver, but still a risk variable, so keep an eye on it.

*

Blood In The Street Account Update

I am planning to sell the “blood in the streets” account today to lock in ~6% return and move money into a fixed/risk-free allocation.

*

So, right now, we are in Step 3 – as market gets extended → lock profits + reduce risk.

And if you miss that shift, that’s exactly how traders give back gains or chase the top.

*

If you enjoyed this video, please ❤️like and 🔁retweet

$VIX spiked to 29.3 in late March. It's now at 18.75.

The market is telling you the fear is gone. But is it?

VIX dropping while geopolitical risk stays elevated and Q1 earnings loom is either a relief rally — or dangerous complacency. Know which one you're in.

$BTC rebounds 5.2% to $74,315 — but it's still down 41% from its Oct 2025 ATH of $126K.

Bounce or bottom?

One session doesn't change the trend. The question is whether this is real accumulation or just relief buying before the next leg down.

DXY just broke below 99 — a 1.25-month low.

Dollar weakness at this scale matters globally: commodities reprice, EM debt gets relief, US exporters benefit.

But a structurally weaker $DXY also signals eroding confidence. That's the part worth watching.

Credit quality. Loan demand. Trading revenue.

If the banks start cracking, the floor cracks with them. Watch closely.
